subject: → Email: CA Biodiversity Project Wrapped
description_or_snippet:
From: ricky@adventurescientists.org
Recipients: sarah.hawkinson@icloud.com

Subject: CA Biodiversity Project Wrapped
Body:

Hi Sarah!

I just saw your insect gear request come in, and I wanted to reach out to let you know we actually wrapped the CA Biodiversity project last month! If you haven't sent any samples to the lab, go ahead and send them in, but we shouldn't send them anymore!

Thanks so much for getting after it, you crushed it! We'll hopefully continue data collection next year, and might even have more projects to come too!

The best way to keep in touch is through our newsletter if you're not a subscriber already. And of course through our social media on Instagram or Facebook!

Thanks again Sarah!

Ricky

Ricky Jones

Senior Manager Data Science

subject: → Email: Application Received — Start Your Training Now! 👏
description_or_snippet:
Hi Matt,

Welcome to Tracking Whitebark Pine and thank you for applying! We’re thrilled to have you join this important effort to monitor and protect high-elevation forests.

Your Volunteer ID is 14127. Be sure to save this number, you will need it to complete the training and submit data out in the field.

Your next step:

👉 Start Training

Since this adventure takes place during winter in snowy backcountry conditions, we recommend both avalanche awareness training and carrying core snow safety gear including, but not limited to: a snow probe, avalanche transceiver, shovel, and offline navigation device.

If you have any questions, reach us anytime at whitebarkpine@adventurescientists.org.

Happy Tracking,
The Adventure Scientists Team

subject: → Email: Don’t Forget — Start Your Whitebark Pine Training
description_or_snippet:
Hi Matt,

Just a quick reminder to start your Tracking Whitebark Pine training today!

It only takes about 20 minutes, and it’s the key to collecting high-quality observations in the field. Aren’t you curious how you can find this high-elevation keystone species that is critical to the entire mountain ecosystem? Once you complete training, you'll get access to all the planning resources to begin you winter adventure.

Slide into the training below:

👉 Start Training

Your Volunteer ID is: 14127

Happy Tracking,
The Adventure Scientists Team

subject: → Email: Can’t wait to see you. Event Details and Final Reminders.
description_or_snippet:
Hey Karen,

Thanks for signing up to join Adventure Scientists and Whitebark Pine Ecosystem Foundation in Bend, Oregon - we’re thrilled to have you along for the celebration.

Here’s everything you need to know:

📅 Thursday, January 8th | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM

Upp Liquids, Bend, Oregon

Come help us celebrate Mt. Bachelor’s certification as a Whitebark Pine Friendly Ski Resort. Meet the teams from Mt Bachelor, Whitebark Pine Ecosystem Foundation, and Adventure Scientists to learn about high-elevation whitebark pine.

We’ll be set up 550 SW Industrial Way #185, Bend, Oregon. Hope to see you there.

BONUS: Take a peek at the Tracking Whitebark Pine Project here

📅 Friday, January 9th | 12:00 PM – 1:00PM/3:00 PM

You’ll need your volunteer ID! Your volunteer ID is: 14215

Lunch & Learn & Look for Whitebark Pine on Mt Bachelor

We’re hosting a lovely lunch out on Mt Bachelor, with a deep dive into whitebark pine and how your winter adventures can help protect this threatened keystone species. Followed by an (optional) outing to ski/snowboard and to visit whitebark pine on Mt Bachelor. Come just for lunch or stay for the entire afternoon —it’s up to you.

Please meet in the West Village Lodge at Mt Bachelor. Staff in the lodge will be happy to help direct you.

🕣 12:00 PM – Lunch & Learn

Join us for a lunch (provided) and a sneak peek at whitebark pine. We’ll also provide on-site training to prepare you to help collect critical scientific data on any/all of your adventures this winter.

🕙 1:00 PM – Look for Whitebark pine.

Come visit the whitebark pine on Mt Bachelor. Together, we’ll track down whitebark pine trees and collect critical data, which will help scientists and land managers protect this keystone species.

How to get here:

The Lunch & Learn will be held on the first floor at West Village Lodge at Mt Bachelor.

13000 SW Century Drive, Bend, OR 97702

There will be an event check-in sign at the entrance. Mt Bachelor staff will be available to help.

Parking: Mt Bachelor has multiple parking lots. Please find more information here. Keep in mind, the event will take place in the West Village Lodge.

✅ What to Bring:

  • Filled, reusable water bottle
  • Ski/snow gear & lift ticket [Purchase HERE] If you’re planning on skiing/boarding with us.
  • If you plan to ski with us, (and haven’t given us more info yet.) make sure to give us more information before you come.
  • Your sense of adventure.

If you have any questions or need to change your plans, send an email to whitebark@adventurescientists.org.

Thanks for being part of this incredible effort to protect this high-alpine keystone species. We can’t wait to see you out there.

Happy Tracking,
Lila and the Adventure Scientists Team

subject: → Email: You're off the waitlist! Mt Bachelor Event Details and Final Reminders
description_or_snippet:
Hey David,

A spot has become available for the Portland event and you're in!

Here’s everything you need to know:

We’re looking forward to seeing you at the Searching for Killer Whales Volunteer Gathering happening on Saturday, January 31st at 4:00 PM.

Below are the final details to help you get ready for a relaxed, fun evening of connecting with fellow volunteers, Adventure Scientists team members, and project partners.

Have you signed up to be a volunteer? Check out more details about the project here.

Note: Space is limited, so if you find that you’re no longer able to attend, please let us know so we can offer your spot to others in the pod.

Event Details

Location:

13th Moon Gravity Well

Address:

4513 SE 41st Ave, Portland, OR 97206, United States

Google Maps Location

Parking

Parking is available at the venue location!

What to Expect

  • First 30 people get a free drink ticket
  • Fun whale-themed trivia
  • A welcoming space to connect with fellow volunteers and collaborators
  • An opportunity to meet some of the Adventure Scientists Team
  • Come ready with whale questions for our partners and advisors

If you have any last-minute questions, don’t hesitate to reach out. Also, if your plans have changed, let us know so we can add someone from the waitlist to the event.

Thank you again for being part of this incredible community. We can’t wait to see you!

Best,

The Adventure Scientists Pod 🐋

subject: → Email: Thank You for Joining Our Killer Whale Community Gathering!
description_or_snippet:
Hi David,

Thank you so much for attending the Searching for Killer Whales in-person project launch! It was wonderful to have you there, and we’re so grateful for the energy, curiosity, and community you brought to the event.

We hope you enjoyed the presentations, jeopardy, and refreshments, and that you left feeling inspired to participate in this important effort to monitor killer whales along our coast.

We’d love to hear your thoughts on the event, what you enjoyed, what we could improve, and how we can better support your involvement moving forward.

Please take 3-5 minutes to complete our short event evaluation.

What's Next:

  • If you have any exciting pictures you would like to share with us and give us permission to upload to our social media if needed feel free to upload them here.
  • Start logging observations: Head to the coast or out on the water to do your favorite outdoor activity, and submit your it in the project app. Remember, even if you do not see any whales, your activity counts. Every data point helps!
  • Stay connected: We’ll continue to share updates, resources, and training opportunities via our weekly newsletter and social media. We would love to hear from you! Did you see something incredible? Did you reconnect with friends to complete your observation? We love hearing about what brought you to the project and what you experienced while volunteering. Don’t be a stranger!
  • Questions? Reach out anytime, we’re here to support your efforts in the field.

We’re thrilled to have you in our pod and look forward to seeing where this project takes us, together.
The Adventure Scientists Team

subject: → Email: Aidan, You're In! Start your Training
description_or_snippet:
Hi Aidan,

Great news! Your application for upcoming adventures in the Pacific Northwest forests has been accepted. Thank you for taking the time to apply and for your enthusiasm to support this conservation effort, we are excited to have you as part of the adventure team. I hope you are as excited to get out and explore the PNW as we are!

Your official Adventure Scientists Volunteer ID is 14622. Be sure to save this number; you will need it to complete the training and submit data out in the field.

Start the training now:

👉 Online Training

Through the training, you will:

  • Prepare for your PNW expedition
  • Learn project protocols and how to collect high-quality data
  • Unlock access to trip planning resources

Once you complete the training, you’ll be prepared to contribute to a region-wide effort to protect Pacific Northwest forest biodiversity, and gain access to the hex locations where your adventure in the field can begin.

We’re grateful for your willingness to step up for this work, and we’re excited to have selected you to be part of this highly sought-after project. We can’t wait to see where your adventure takes you.

Happy adventuring,

Samantha with Adventure Scientists

subject: → Email: Application Received — Start Your Training Now! 👏
description_or_snippet:
Hi Kari,

Welcome to Tracking Whitebark Pine and thank you for applying! We’re thrilled to have you join this important effort to monitor and protect high-elevation forests.

Your Volunteer ID is 14962. Be sure to save this number, you will need it to complete the training and submit data out in the field.

Your next step:

👉 Start Training

Since this adventure takes place during winter in snowy backcountry conditions, we recommend both avalanche awareness training and carrying core snow safety gear including, but not limited to: a snow probe, avalanche transceiver, shovel, and offline navigation device.

If you have any questions, reach us anytime at whitebarkpine@adventurescientists.org.

Happy Tracking,
The Adventure Scientists Team

subject: → Email: Julia, You're In! Start your Training
description_or_snippet:
Hi Julia,

Great news! Your application for upcoming adventures in the Pacific Northwest forests has been accepted. Thank you for taking the time to apply and for your enthusiasm to support this conservation effort, we are excited to have you as part of the adventure team. I hope you are as excited to get out and explore the PNW as we are!

Your official Adventure Scientists Volunteer ID is 15154. Be sure to save this number; you will need it to complete the training and submit data out in the field.

Start the training now:

👉 Online Training

Through the training, you will:

  • Prepare for your PNW expedition
  • Learn project protocols and how to collect high-quality data
  • Unlock access to trip planning resources

Once you complete the training, you’ll be prepared to contribute to a region-wide effort to protect Pacific Northwest forest biodiversity, and gain access to the hex locations where your adventure in the field can begin.

We’re grateful for your willingness to step up for this work, and we’re excited to have selected you to be part of this highly sought-after project. We can’t wait to see where your adventure takes you.

Happy adventuring,

Samantha with Adventure Scientists

subject: Can’t wait to see you. Event Details and Final Reminders.
description_or_snippet:
Hey Karen,

Thanks for signing up to join Adventure Scientists and Whitebark Pine Ecosystem Foundation in Bend, Oregon - we’re thrilled to have you along for the celebration.

Here’s everything you need to know:

📅 Thursday, January 8th | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM

Upp Liquids, Bend, Oregon

Come help us celebrate Mt. Bachelor’s certification as a Whitebark Pine Friendly Ski Resort. Meet the teams from Mt Bachelor, Whitebark Pine Ecosystem Foundation, and Adventure Scientists to learn about high-elevation whitebark pine.

We’ll be set up 550 SW Industrial Way #185, Bend, Oregon. Hope to see you there.

BONUS: Take a peek at the Tracking Whitebark Pine Project here

📅 Friday, January 9th | 12:00 PM – 1:00PM/3:00 PM

You’ll need your volunteer ID! Your volunteer ID is: 14215

Lunch & Learn & Look for Whitebark Pine on Mt Bachelor

We’re hosting a lovely lunch out on Mt Bachelor, with a deep dive into whitebark pine and how your winter adventures can help protect this threatened keystone species. Followed by an (optional) outing to ski/snowboard and to visit whitebark pine on Mt Bachelor. Come just for lunch or stay for the entire afternoon —it’s up to you.

Please meet in the West Village Lodge at Mt Bachelor. Staff in the lodge will be happy to help direct you.

🕣 12:00 PM – Lunch & Learn

Join us for a lunch (provided) and a sneak peek at whitebark pine. We’ll also provide on-site training to prepare you to help collect critical scientific data on any/all of your adventures this winter.

🕙 1:00 PM – Look for Whitebark pine.

Come visit the whitebark pine on Mt Bachelor. Together, we’ll track down whitebark pine trees and collect critical data, which will help scientists and land managers protect this keystone species.

How to get here:

The Lunch & Learn will be held on the first floor at West Village Lodge at Mt Bachelor.

13000 SW Century Drive, Bend, OR 97702

There will be an event check-in sign at the entrance. Mt Bachelor staff will be available to help.

Parking: Mt Bachelor has multiple parking lots. Please find more information here. Keep in mind, the event will take place in the West Village Lodge.

✅ What to Bring:

  • Filled, reusable water bottle
  • Ski/snow gear & lift ticket [Purchase HERE] If you’re planning on skiing/boarding with us.
  • If you plan to ski with us, (and haven’t given us more info yet.) make sure to give us more information before you come.
  • Your sense of adventure.

If you have any questions or need to change your plans, send an email to whitebark@adventurescientists.org.

Thanks for being part of this incredible effort to protect this high-alpine keystone species. We can’t wait to see you out there.

Happy Tracking,
Lila and the Adventure Scientists Team

subject: You're off the waitlist! Mt Bachelor Event Details and Final Reminders
description_or_snippet:
Hey David,

A spot has become available for the Portland event and you're in!

Here’s everything you need to know:

We’re looking forward to seeing you at the Searching for Killer Whales Volunteer Gathering happening on Saturday, January 31st at 4:00 PM.

Below are the final details to help you get ready for a relaxed, fun evening of connecting with fellow volunteers, Adventure Scientists team members, and project partners.

Have you signed up to be a volunteer? Check out more details about the project here.

Note: Space is limited, so if you find that you’re no longer able to attend, please let us know so we can offer your spot to others in the pod.

Event Details

Location:

13th Moon Gravity Well

Address:

4513 SE 41st Ave, Portland, OR 97206, United States

Google Maps Location

Parking

Parking is available at the venue location!

What to Expect

  • First 30 people get a free drink ticket
  • Fun whale-themed trivia
  • A welcoming space to connect with fellow volunteers and collaborators
  • An opportunity to meet some of the Adventure Scientists Team
  • Come ready with whale questions for our partners and advisors

If you have any last-minute questions, don’t hesitate to reach out. Also, if your plans have changed, let us know so we can add someone from the waitlist to the event.

Thank you again for being part of this incredible community. We can’t wait to see you!

Best,

The Adventure Scientists Pod 🐋

subject: Thank You for Joining Our Killer Whale Community Gathering!
description_or_snippet:
Hi David,

Thank you so much for attending the Searching for Killer Whales in-person project launch! It was wonderful to have you there, and we’re so grateful for the energy, curiosity, and community you brought to the event.

We hope you enjoyed the presentations, jeopardy, and refreshments, and that you left feeling inspired to participate in this important effort to monitor killer whales along our coast.

We’d love to hear your thoughts on the event, what you enjoyed, what we could improve, and how we can better support your involvement moving forward.

Please take 3-5 minutes to complete our short event evaluation.

What's Next:

  • If you have any exciting pictures you would like to share with us and give us permission to upload to our social media if needed feel free to upload them here.
  • Start logging observations: Head to the coast or out on the water to do your favorite outdoor activity, and submit your it in the project app. Remember, even if you do not see any whales, your activity counts. Every data point helps!
  • Stay connected: We’ll continue to share updates, resources, and training opportunities via our weekly newsletter and social media. We would love to hear from you! Did you see something incredible? Did you reconnect with friends to complete your observation? We love hearing about what brought you to the project and what you experienced while volunteering. Don’t be a stranger!
  • Questions? Reach out anytime, we’re here to support your efforts in the field.

We’re thrilled to have you in our pod and look forward to seeing where this project takes us, together.
The Adventure Scientists Team

subject: Application Received — Start Your Training Now! 👏
description_or_snippet:
Hi Kari,

Welcome to Tracking Whitebark Pine and thank you for applying! We’re thrilled to have you join this important effort to monitor and protect high-elevation forests.

Your Volunteer ID is 14962. Be sure to save this number, you will need it to complete the training and submit data out in the field.

Your next step:

👉 Start Training

Since this adventure takes place during winter in snowy backcountry conditions, we recommend both avalanche awareness training and carrying core snow safety gear including, but not limited to: a snow probe, avalanche transceiver, shovel, and offline navigation device.

If you have any questions, reach us anytime at whitebarkpine@adventurescientists.org.

Happy Tracking,
The Adventure Scientists Team
